data.prompt_text: Two radar stations at B and C are 126 kilometers apart along a straight coastline. They track a ship at sea at point A. The angle \angle B is measured as 25.5^\circ and \angle C is 53.9^\circ. Find the distance from station B to the ship (distance AB).
data.solution_steps:
\text{We are given side } a = 126 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 25.5^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 53.9^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 25.5^\circ - 53.9^\circ = 100.6^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(53.9^\circ)} = \frac{126}{\sin(100.6^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{126\sin(53.9^\circ)}{\sin(100.6^\circ)}

c \approx 103.6

c \approx 399.4

c \approx 105.0

\text{We are given side } a = 48 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 111.8^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 34.2^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 111.8^\circ - 34.2^\circ = 34.0^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(34.2^\circ)} = \frac{48}{\sin(34.0^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{48\sin(34.2^\circ)}{\sin(34.0^\circ)}

c \approx 48.2

\text{We are given side } a = 35 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 111.4^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 17.0^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 111.4^\circ - 17.0^\circ = 51.6^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(17.0^\circ)} = \frac{35}{\sin(51.6^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{35\sin(17.0^\circ)}{\sin(51.6^\circ)}

c \approx 13.1

data.solution_steps:
\text{We are given side } a = 336 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 20.0^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 129.7^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 20.0^\circ - 129.7^\circ = 30.3^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(129.7^\circ)} = \frac{336}{\sin(30.3^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{336\sin(129.7^\circ)}{\sin(30.3^\circ)}

c \approx 512.4
